message:
fcp-host: X-Principal header binding + connector zone pinning (br-t623k, br-by4vu)

br-flywheel_connectors-t623k [LOW]
  HTTP invoke_handler previously passed principal_override=None to
  evaluate_live_preflight, so the HTTP layer could not bind the
  caller's asserted identity to the capability token's subject/
  principal_id claim. The capability-based model treats the token
  AS the identity — correct by design — but it leaves no room for
  defense-in-depth when an upstream proxy has already authenticated
  the caller.

  Add an optional X-Principal header:
    * extract_principal_header(&HeaderMap) -> Option<String>
      reads, trims, and drops empty / non-UTF-8 values.
    * invoke_handler now takes HeaderMap and forwards the value as
      principal_override; verify_live_request already rejects when
      the override does not match the token's principal claim.
    * 5 unit tests cover the extractor's happy, trimmed, empty,
      non-UTF-8, and absent paths.

  Back-compat: existing clients that do not send X-Principal keep
  the pre-fix behavior (token's claim drives the principal).

br-flywheel_connectors-by4vu [HIGH]
  The gateway previously did not consult any connector-side zone
  declaration when routing invoke requests — a valid capability
  token signed for zone Z let the holder invoke *any* connector
  whose operations matched the token's cap_id, regardless of the
  zone the connector was declared to serve. Cross-zone invocation
  was achievable with a structurally-valid token in the wrong
  request.zone_id.

  Fix:
    * ManagedConnectorConfig gains an `allowed_zones: Vec<String>`
      field (default empty for back-compat).
    * SubprocessRegistry::allowed_zones(connector_id) looks the
      list up per-entry.
    * verify_live_request now rejects with a distinct PreflightFailed
      message when allowed_zones is non-empty and request.zone_id
      is not in the set. Empty preserves pre-binding behavior so
      existing inventories don't break.
    * New integration-style test verify_live_request_rejects_zone_
      outside_connector_binding exercises the fail-closed path with
      a real spawned connector subprocess.

  Operators should populate allowed_zones for every production
  connector to match the manifest's [zones] home / allowed_sources.

Files: crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs (+10 lines),
       crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs (+220 lines).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
